question: In Tutor Tube Video Editor, what happens when you finalize the export after enabling 'Separate files', and how are the output files named?
answer: After selecting the 'Separate files' option and clicking 'Export', the file explorer window shows newly created files named with timestamp ranges, such as "San Francisco Townhouses - Copy-00:00:06.835-00:00:12.796.mp4" and "San Francisco Townhouses - Copy-00:00:12.796-00:00:21.672.mp4".
keypoints: The user selects the 'checkbox' next to the 'Separate files' option to export each segment as an individual file.
The user clicks on the 'Export' button once more to generate separate files for each segment.
The file explorer window updates to show the new files named "San Francisco Townhouses - Copy-00:00:06.835-00:00:12.796.mp4" and "San Francisco Townhouses - Copy-00:00:12.796-00:00:21.672.mp4".

question: In Tutor Tube Video Editor, what notification appears after merging cuts with 'Enable Feature X' activated, and how do you dismiss it?
answer: After enabling 'Enable Feature X' in Settings and clicking 'Merge cuts', a notification appears stating: "Done! Note: cutpoints may be inaccurate. Make sure you test the output files..." Dismiss it by clicking the 'Close' button in the dialog box.
keypoints: The user selects the checkbox next to the 'Enable Feature X' label within the 'Settings' window to enable additional export options.
The user clicks on the 'Merge cuts' button to combine multiple segments into a single clip.

The screen displays a notification stating "Done! Note: cutpoints may be inaccurate. Make sure you test the output files in your desired player/editor before you delete the source. If output does not look right, see the HELP page." after the merge operation.
The user clicks on the 'Close' button in the notification dialog box to dismiss the message.
